title: After less than a year, Vodafone takes back control in UK
summary: Vodafone Group is buying CK Hutchison's 49% stake in VodafoneThree, raising questions about brand strategies as well as the Hong Kong group's telecoms strategy in Europe.

title: Satellite still a 'complement' for overall connectivity, Verizon exec says
summary: Satellite connectivity is in the headlines and driving a lot of hype, but its overall role remains dwarfed by terrestrial wireless and wireline networks, says Verizon's Srini Kalapala.

summary: Verizon private 5G networks to power World Cup referee bodycams and the carrier adds three- to five-times more capacity in and around 16 stadiums for the event.

title: What T-Mobile stopped telling you about Q1 2026
summary: T-Mobile quietly dropped its key growth metrics in Q1. Analyst Roger Entner rebuilds the numbers and explains what the silence is hiding.

title: Charter offers second line on same mobile device
summary: Spectrum Mobile, Charter's mobile service, now lets customers add a dedicated voice and text number to compatible smartphones for an additional $10 per month. #pressrelease

title: Shane West to take helm of Allo Fiber
summary: Allo Fiber said former TDS COO Shane West will succeed Brad Moline as CEO, effective June 1. Moline will remain on the Allo board and serve as president – leased networks.

title: FCC to implement 'overly burdensome' E-Rate bidding portal
summary: The FCC last week voted to introduce a new competitive bidding portal for E-Rate program participants. School and library advocates called the change 'unnecessary' and 'overly burdensome.'

summary: Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: Vodafone does branded communications with First Orion; Ookla unpicks network congestion; Neos Networks helps deliver sovereign edge cloud.

title: ADB's $70B program will link power grids and digital networks across Asia-Pacific
summary: The Asian Development Bank has launched the Pan-Asia Power Grid Initiative and the Asia-Pacific Digital Highway, which aim to transform regional connectivity by linking power and digital networks.

summary: Japanese telco exports its hyper-personalized digital-only model.

title: Shane West named CEO of Allo Fiber
summary: Former TDS COO Shane West is succeeding Brad Moline as CEO of Allo Fiber, effective June 1. Moline will become president-leased networks and continue to serve on Allo's board. #pressrelease

title: Charter taps Rodrian to lead new connectivity products team
summary: Charter has promoted Dave Rodrian to SVP, connectivity products, where he now oversees a newly formed group that heads up strategy and development for the operator's Internet, Wi-Fi and voice products.

title: AirTrunk and PDG expand data center footprint in Southeast Asia
summary: AirTrunk and Princeton Digital Group have announced plans for new hyperscale data center facilities in Malaysia and Indonesia, respectively.

title: India's 6G ambition faces a 5G monetization reality check
summary: India has world-class 5G infrastructure, but low ARPU, a tiny base of premium users and digitally immature enterprises could threaten its 6G aspirations.

title: Crown Castle closes sale of fiber and small cell units
summary: Crown Castle has closed the sale of its fiber business to Zayo Group and its small cell business to Arium Networks. #pressrelease

title: Cable One seeing inconsistent competition from Starlink
summary: Cable One is seeing haphazard competition from Starlink, as pricing and offers have varied from market to market, but views satellite broadband as a 'formidable competitor,' says CEO Jim Holanda.

title: Ruckus sale could lead to more cable tech consolidation
summary: Vistance Networks, the former CommScope, is selling its Ruckus unit to Belden for $1.84 billion. Vistance will retain its cable and PON unit, Aurora Networks, and look to grow it via possible M&A.

title: The Buildout: Michigan WOWed by more fiber connections
summary: This week in broadband builds: WideOpenWest (WOW) connects 17,000 more locations in Michigan; Comcast comes to Bloomsburg, Pennsylvania; Arvig to extend Minnesota fiber lines; New York names its BEAD winners – and more.
